rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE MORALES et al VS. CLUB ONE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Of Hearing On X-Deft Michelle Morales' Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Following Order (Ccp 425.16)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 7, 2008 LINE 15. PLAINTIFF MICHELLE MORALES' (Re-Notice) Of Hearing On CROSS-DEFENDANT Michelle Morales' Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Following Order (C.C.P. SECTION 425.16) IS GRANTED. COUNSEL'S HOURLY RATES ARE SUPPORTED BY THE RECORD AS IS THE ENHANCEMENT OF 1.2 FOR THE WORK DONE ON THE MOTION, NOT FOR FEES. THE COURT HAS REDUCED PLAINTIFFS' HOURS BY TEN PER CENT IN CONNECTION WITH THE MOTION AND HAS INCLUDED THE TIME SPENT OPPOSING THE MOTION FOR RELIEF. THE AWARD IS: $39,042 IN CONNECTION WITH THE MOTION AND $9,650 IN CONNECTION WITH THE FEE REQUEST. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE MORALES et al VS. CLUB ONE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Itsorder Granting Pltf'S Anti-Slapp Motion;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 7, 2008 LINE 15. DEFENDANT ANGELO ORTEGA'S Motion For Relief From Its order Granting PLAINTIFF'S Anti-SLAPP Motion IS DENIED. THIS IS A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND IT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P. SECTION 1008. RELIEF MAY NOT BE GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 473(B). THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING AS TO WHY THE MCBRIDE DECLARATION WAS NOT OBTAINED IN CONNECTION WITH DEFENDANT'S INITIAL OPPOSITION. MR. MCBRIDE'S STATEMENT AS TO WHAT THE CLUB ASSISTANT MANAGER STATED IS HEARSAY. PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IS ADOPTED BUT FOR PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION THREE AND THE ALTERNATIVE LANGUAGE HAS BEEN DELETED. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KATIUSKA ABURTO et al VS. ANNA T WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 7, 2008 LINE 13. PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THE AMENDED DECLARATION OF ORTIZ COMPLIES WITH C.C.P. SECTION 2015.5 AND PLAINTIFF'S HAVE PROVIDED COMPETENT EVIDENCE OF A VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 789.3 AND ARE ENTITLED TO INJUNCTIVE RELIEF UNDER SUBSECTION D. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CIVIL CODE SECTION 827 IN SEEKING TO CHANGE THE TERMS OF TENANCY. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE EVIDENCE GENERALLY ARE DENIED, BUT FOR CERTAIN PORTIONS OF THE O'BRIEN DECLARATION. =(302/PJM/PB)
